channel.title: Development on Ethan Ligon
channel.description: Recent content in Development on Ethan Ligon

item.title: Assessing Targeting Performance: The Case of Ghana's LEAP Program
item.description: Evaluates targeting accuracy of Ghana's LEAP social protection program using marginal utility of expenditure as a welfare benchmark.

item.title: Spatial Procurement of Farm Products and the Supply of Processed Foods
item.description: Develops a spatial model of farm product procurement by food processors, applied to California's tomato processing industry.

item.title: Inferring Informal Risk-Sharing Regimes: Evidence from Rural Tanzania
item.description: Exploits the link between interest rates and consumption moments to test alternative risk-sharing models without interest rate data.

item.title: What Explains Low Adoption of Digital Payment Technologies?
item.description: Investigates why small-scale merchants in Jaipur, India have low adoption rates for digital payment technologies despite minimal supply-side barriers.

item.title: Estimating the Relative Benefits of Agricultural Growth on the Distribution of Expenditures
item.description: Estimates the relative benefits of agricultural versus non-agricultural GDP growth on household expenditures across the income distribution.

item.title: Poverty and the Welfare Costs of Risk Associated with Globalization
item.description: Uses 50 years of Lorenz curve data to measure the welfare costs of risk borne by poor households and whether globalization increases that risk.

item.title: Formal Markets and Informal Insurance
item.description: Examines how the introduction of formal credit markets may undermine informal insurance arrangements in developing-country villages.

item.title: Economic Distance and Cross-Country Spillovers
item.description: Estimates the magnitude of cross-country growth spillovers using a novel measure of economic distance between nations.

item.title: Government Management of Village Commons
item.description: Compares two government policies for managing village forest commons when direct enforcement is difficult.

item.title: Liquidity Constraints and Incentive Contracts
item.description: Develops methods to distinguish whether firms and households face exogenous credit constraints or endogenous constraints arising from moral hazard or limited commitment.

item.title: Risk-Sharing and Information in Village Economies
item.description: Tests the implications of private information for risk sharing in ICRISAT villages, finding that information problems constrain consumption smoothing.

item.title: Computing the Equilibria of Dynamic Common Property Games
item.description: Develops computational methods for finding equilibria of dynamic games in which multiple agents exploit a common-pool resource.

item.title: The Swamp and the Shopping Center: An Interest Rate Parable
item.description: Uses a parable of a swamp and a shopping center to illustrate how the choice of discount rate affects cost-benefit analysis of environmental projects.
